input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI ContentPage called SettingsPage.xaml that currently uses
plain bindings with no x:DataType. The page's BindingContext is a
SettingsViewModel with properties: string Theme, bool NotificationsEnabled,
and int FontSize. Convert this page to use compiled bindings and tell me
how to catch binding errors at build time.

data.content: There are no files in the working directory, so I'll create a before/after example and explain the steps.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.SettingsPage"
             Title="Settings">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Theme" />
        <Entry Text="{Binding Theme}" />

        <Label Text="Enable Notifications" />
        <Switch IsToggled="{Binding NotificationsEnabled}" />

        <Label Text="Font Size" />
        <Slider Minimum="8" Maximum="32"
                Value="{Binding FontSize}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.SettingsPage"
             x:DataType="vm:SettingsViewModel"
             Title="Settings">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Theme" />
        <Entry Text="{Binding Theme}" />

        <Label Text="Enable Notifications" />
        <Switch IsToggled="{Binding NotificationsEnabled}" />

        <Label Text="Font Size" />
        <Slider Minimum="8" Maximum="32"
                Value="{Binding FontSize}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>

Since there are no files to work with, I'll create example files to demonstrate the conversion.

data.content:
Two changes convert your page to compiled bindings:

### 1. Add the XML namespace for your ViewModel
```xml
xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
```

### 2. Add `x:DataType` to the root element
```xml
x:DataType="vm:SettingsViewModel"
```

That's it — your existing `{Binding …}` expressions stay the same. The XAML compiler now resolves `Theme`, `NotificationsEnabled`, and `FontSize` against `SettingsViewModel` **at compile time**. A typo like `{Binding Themes}` will produce a **build error**, not a silent runtime failure.

### Catching errors at build time

- **Errors appear automatically** — with `x:DataType` set, any binding to a nonexistent or mistyped property becomes a compiler error (e.g., `XC0045`).
- If you ever need to opt out for a specific binding (e.g., a `BindingContext` set dynamically), use `x:DataType=""` on that element to disable compiled bindings locally.
- You can set `x:DataType` on **any** element in the tree (not just the page root), useful for `DataTemplate` inside `CollectionView`/`ListView`.

The created files in your working directory show the before (`SettingsPage_Before.xaml`) and after (`SettingsPage.xaml`).
